checks.finding: All AC-001 through AC-016 referenced by tasks.md resolve to criteria in acceptance-tests.md.
All four tasks use either literal None or comma-separated valid task IDs; blockers_format_valid is true.
Every REQ-001 through REQ-017 appears in at least one task Requirements field.
Every AC-001 through AC-016 is assigned across T-001 through T-004.
Each task references at least one valid REQ-NNN identifier.
No task title or scope defines an exclusively test/spec/verify task requiring separate orphan-test treatment.
In round 2, T-001 through T-004 remain Approval: Draft and Status: Planned.
T-001 medium→acceptance-first, T-002 and T-003 high→tdd, and T-004 low→test-after match the risk matrix; precheck also reports PASS.
Primary AC assignments are disjoint: T-001 owns AC-001–AC-010, T-002 AC-011–AC-013, T-003 AC-015–AC-016, and T-004 AC-014.
Every blocker reference resolves to a node among T-001, T-002, T-003, and T-004 in dependency-graph.json.
The dependency graph is acyclic: T-003 waits for T-001, while T-004 waits for T-001, T-002, and T-003.
Each task has one coherent concern: layer artifacts, structure validation, review-gate binding, or release metadata.

In tasks.md, T-001 Done When item 'Layer-local unknowns and unaffected bugfix/refactor layers have explicit reasoned values' names no exact artifact, fixture, command, or evidence location. T-002 and T-003 each say rollback 'is exercised' against fixtures without stating the expected observable result. These criteria leave downstream completion ambiguous; this is Major because every Done When item must be independently inspectable and measurable.
traceability.md contains entries for T-001 through T-004, and all listed requirement identifiers resolve to REQ-001 through REQ-017.
